title: Jesuits expelled from the Spanish Empire
blurb: Charles III orders the Society of Jesus removed from Spain and all its colonies.

title: Fall of Ayutthaya
blurb: Burmese armies sack the Siamese capital, ending a kingdom of four centuries.

title: The Townshend Acts
blurb: Britain taxes its American colonies anew, deepening the road to revolution.

title: Catherine II's Legislative Commission
blurb: The Russian empress convenes reformers to recodify the law, guided by her Nakaz.

title: First Anglo-Mysore War
blurb: Hyder Ali's Mysore takes on the British East India Company in southern India.

title: Mason–Dixon line completed
blurb: Surveyors fix the famous boundary between Pennsylvania and Maryland.

title: The Qing–Burma War rages
blurb: Qing China's third invasion of Burma is repulsed in the frontier jungles.

title: Cook's Endeavour sails
blurb: A rival British Pacific expedition departs Plymouth as Bougainville crosses the ocean.

title: Gorkha conquest of Kathmandu
blurb: Prithvi Narayan Shah takes the valley, founding the kingdom of Nepal.

title: Russo-Turkish War begins
blurb: The Ottoman Empire declares war on Russia, a struggle that will run to 1774.

title: Encyclopædia Britannica begins
blurb: The first weekly instalments of the encyclopaedia appear in Edinburgh.

title: Royal Academy of Arts founded
blurb: London gains its academy of painting and sculpture, Joshua Reynolds presiding.

title: Watt patents the steam condenser
blurb: James Watt's separate condenser points the age toward steam power.
